title: Guns of Glory - Tips for Protecting Yourself
content:
This guide will explain the various ways to protect or defend yourself against enemy (rally) attacks in Guns of Glory. Game Favours Attackers The game favours attackers. Let's as an example look at a situation of two alliances each with few big players with buildings level 20, and the rest small players with buildings level 15. When one of the big players leads a rally against a smaller player, the big player can have a rally size of 500,000 with a Hall of War lvl 20, which can be filled by fellow alliance members, big and small. Looking at a small player it is a lot more difficult to get an army of 500,000 troops to defend yourself at level 15. Training so many troops yourself will become more and more difficult as you have more troops due to the upkeep. An Embassy level 15 only allows your alliance members to reinforce you with 90,000 troops. The Trap Capacity for a Trap Factory level 15 is 22,500. This means even if you have been able to build an army of 90,000 your defenders are outnumbered significantly. The amount of troops are an important factor, but the level 20 rally leader also would normally be able to have much better combat boosts from Airship Abilities, Lord Talent Points Projects, Research Projects, and equipment, so the attacking troops would also have much better stats. Realize that the combined effect of these can be quite huge. If you are online when attacked, you still have the option to use some items to increase Attack and Defense, but if not, this is also favoring the Attacker. The above means that unless you really have a huge army and you are fully reinforced, you do not stand a chance against incoming rally attacks. The difference is so huge that the attacker can wipe out most castles without taking significant losses. You do have a benefit as defender, as long as you have hospital capacity your lost troops will not die but end up in your hospitals (or alliance hospital), while the attacking troops that are lost (except for the battle treatment percentage) will die. Shield The only way to really project yourself is to use a peace shield. Putting up a Peace Shield means nobody can attack you, so your resources and troops are save. However peace shields are not cheap (e.g. an 8 hour peace shield costs 600 gold or 60,000 alliance loyalty), and kill events last 3 days, not even considering attacks outside of kill events. It is going to be expensive to stay shielded whenever you are not online. Defending against a Rally/Attack When an attack is incoming and you are online and you do not put up a shield, you can chose to defend against the attack. Depending on your Watchtower level you get information about the attack. This can help you in deciding whether defending against the attack is the best option. If you decide to defend you can consider the following: ask your alliance members to reinforce your embassy, you can make sure your equipment and lord talent points are set for combat, and you can use items to boost your attack and defense stats. Keep in mind that the attacker is online for sure and can call off the attack. Consider your hospital capacity and alliance hospital capacity, make sure you have enough to store all your troops, you might need it. Emptying Your Castle When an attack is incoming you can also opt to empty your castle. This means send your troops outside and make sure the resources you have are below Storehouse levels. You can use your resources by for example starting a building upgrade, research project and troop trainings. You can also decide to send some to the alliance storehouse or aliance mates. You can send out your troops to gather resources, reinforce an alliance member, or garrison one of the alliance buildings. Even attacking a monster if you make sure it keeps them away from your castle long enough is ok. Your traps will not engage in battle when there are no troops in your castle. They will not inflict any kills, but also not get damaged. Strategy Center The Strategy Center Alliance Building offers some strategies to defend the alliance, which can help against incoming attacks as well. Most useful is probably the Alliance Shielding Strategy that casts a 4 hour shield to all alliance members. Before you go offline When you are not online you do not have the option to react on incoming attacks, you need to make a decision in advance. As stated before, staying shielded whenever you are offline is expensive and for most players not an option. Because of the inbalance between attack and defense leaving your troops in your castle can be costly. Sure, if you have enough hospital capacity none of your troops will die, but it still takes a lot of resources to heal your troops, and a lot of time. However not leaving your troops in your castle has some disadvantages as well, it means people can just attack you and take your excess resources, making it difficult to build up resources for larger building upgrades and research projects. It is a difficult choice to make, but basically you will probably decide to not keep your troops in your castle anymore if you are hit too often and have to heal all your troops. If that is the case you might opt to send your troops to an alliance member that is shielded (or a very strong alliance member if no one puts up a shield). If you have sufficient troops and march size it might be a good option to send a full march to the alliance resource building as well. You can even temporarily change your airship abilities to lower (meaning remove any abilities that increase gathering speed) resource gathering speed and increase march capacity and troop load, so that your troops will take longer to come back from the alliance resource building. Sending troops to Garrison an alliance building is not safe, especially the fortress can be a target and if others notice your troops in garrison in another building they can target it as well. Garrison seems to work like a rally, so if you use it it is important that the strongest player (best boosts) will Garrison first. Conclusion The best way to protect yourself is putting up a shield, but since that is quite expensive you might decide to reinforce a shielded alliance member, or send your troops out gathering to the alliance resource building, before you go offline for longer times. If you have any questions or other tips to defend or protect yourself, please leave a comment.

They will not inflict any kills, but also not get damaged. Strategy Center The Strategy Center Alliance Building offers some strategies to defend the alliance, which can help against incoming attacks as well. Most useful is probably the Alliance Shielding Strategy that casts a 4 hour shield to all alliance members. Before you go offline When you are not online you do not have the option to react on incoming attacks, you need to make a decision in advance. As stated before, staying shielded whenever you are offline is expensive and for most players not an option. Because of the inbalance between attack and defense leaving your troops in your castle can be costly. Sure, if you have enough hospital capacity none of your troops will die, but it still takes a lot of resources to heal your troops, and a lot of time. However not leaving your troops in your castle has some disadvantages as well, it means people can just attack you and take your excess resources, making it difficult to build up resources for larger building upgrades and research projects. It is a difficult choice to make, but basically you will probably decide to not keep your troops in your castle anymore if you are hit too often and have to heal all your troops. If that is the case you might opt to send your troops to an alliance member that is shielded (or a very strong alliance member if no one puts up a shield). If you have sufficient troops and march size it might be a good option to send a full march to the alliance resource building as well. You can even temporarily change your airship abilities to lower (meaning remove any abilities that increase gathering speed) resource gathering speed and increase march capacity and troop load, so that your troops will take longer to come back from the alliance resource building. Sending troops to Garrison an alliance building is not safe, especially the fortress can be a target and if others notice your troops in garrison in another building they can target it as well. Garrison seems to work like a rally, so if you use it it is important that the strongest player (best boosts) will Garrison first. Conclusion The best way to protect yourself is putting up a shield, but since that is quite expensive you might decide to reinforce a shielded alliance member, or send your troops out gathering to the alliance resource building, before you go offline for longer times. If you have any questions or other tips to defend or protect yourself, please leave a comment.

Consider your hospital capacity and alliance hospital capacity, make sure you have enough to store all your troops, you might need it. Emptying Your Castle When an attack is incoming you can also opt to empty your castle. This means send your troops outside and make sure the resources you have are below Storehouse levels. You can use your resources by for example starting a building upgrade, research project and troop trainings. You can also decide to send some to the alliance storehouse or aliance mates. title: Guns of Glory - Retribution vs Demon Hunter Analysis
content:
My advice for other players is to stay away from Retribution until it becomes free like Demon Hunter did. Maybe another two months. LOL Closing Thought I would like to thank Boris Pore to contributing this, great stuff that I would never have been able to figure out without spending big. title: Guns of Glory - Sun King vs Master
content:
The Sun King and Master pieces can be combined, but not every piece of the newer set is an improvement over the old set. In this article we explain the difference and share some considerations for combining pieces of the different sets. Introduction Sun King and Master pieces can be combined, because they are all part of the Royal Academy of Warfare, which means that if you combine pieces of the different sets, you still get the set boosts. If you have enough Ingot, Materials and Steel you should of course forge all Master pieces and enhance them to +5, but many players will not have enough, end up with a mix, in which case it can make a large difference which pieces you forge. Below we will first look at the Stats of each pieces, so that you can already see the key differences. Afterwards we make an analysis that should help you determine which pieces to forge. Stats Find below for each of the 6 pieces a comparison of the stats. It shows Sun King unenhanced (SK0), Sun King +5 (SK5), Master unenhanced (M0), Master +5 (M5). 
For our analysis below it is important to look at the columns SK5 and M0. To emphasize the difference we have shown the higher values in the columns SK5 and M0 in bold. Armor Boost SK0 SK5 M0 M5 Cavalry Health 65% 91% 95% 141% Distance Health 65% 91% 100% 148.5% Troop Health 65% 81.25% 85% 126% Troop Defense 60% 75% 0% 0% Infantry Health 65% 91% 100% 148.5% Artillery Defense 0% 0% 95% 141% Boots Boost SK0 SK5 M0 M5 Artillery Health 130% 182% 190% 282% Artillery Defense 65% 91% 0% 0% Distance Attack 65% 91% 90% 133.5% Distance Defense 55% 77% 80% 119% Troop Health 55% 68.75% 67.5% 100% Troop Defense 0% 0% 80% 119% Headgear Boost SK0 SK5 M0 M5 Cavalry Health 65% 91% 95% 141% Cavalry Defense 65% 91% 95% 141% Troop Health 30% 37.5% 42.5% 63% Troop Attack 50% 62.5% 60% 89% Infantry Defense 65% 91% 100% 148.5% Weapon Boost SK0 SK5 M0 M5 Cavalry Attack 65% 91% 90% 133.5% Distance Attack 65% 91% 100% 148.5% Troop Defense 60% 75% 60% 89% Infantry Attack 130% 182% 190% 282% Infantry Defense 65% 91% 90% 133.5% Necklace Boost SK0 SK5 M0 M5 Distance Health 65% 91% 90% 133.5% Distance Defense 75% 105% 110% 163.5% Troop Attack 50% 62.5% 47.5% 70.5% Troop Defense 30% 37.5% 55% 81.5% Infantry Health 65% 91% 90% 133.5% Ring Boost SK0 SK5 M0 M5 Cavalry Attack 65% 91% 100% 148.5% Cavalry Defense 65% 91% 95% 141% Artillery Attack 130% 182% 190% 282% Troop Attack 50% 62.5% 87.5% 130% Artillery Defense 65% 91% 95% 141% Analysis Based on the above differences for the SK5 to M0 upgrade and weight I assigned to the various boosts (e.g. I don't assign any value to Artillery boosts) I have calculated a value for upgrading each of the pieces, see table below. SK5 - M0 Value Assignment Piece Upgrade Points Armor -104 Boots 153 Headgeat 38.5 Weapon -15 Necklace -13 Ring 88 This means the order in which you should forge Master pieces is Boots, Ring, Headgear, Necklace, Weapon and Armor. The very important point is to keep an eye on Boots and Armor. For Boots Artillery Defense is replaced by Troop Defense, which is very good, and for Armor the opposite, Troop Defense is replaced by Artillery Defense, which is clearly very bad. Ring is a good second. It scores high mostly because of the Troop Attack, which I value a lot. Headgear still has a positive balance, but for the others you might want to wait with an upgrade until you can enhance the Master piece. Especially for Armor, for which the Master needs to be enhanced to +3 before it can be considered an improvement over SK5. Conclusion Not sure why they setup the game like this, but pay attention to the boosts when upgrading, as explained, it can make a significant difference.

Conclusion Not sure why they setup the game like this, but pay attention to the boosts when upgrading, as explained, it can make a significant difference.

title: How to bind or link account to Facebook or Google
content:
How do I bind/link my account to Facebook or Google?

In the game, tap your avatar at the top-left corner, then select Settings. In the Account Settings section, you can choose from the supported platforms (Facebook, Google, etc.) to bind or link your account.

title: How to transfer account to a new phone
content:
I switched to a new phone, how do I transfer my account?

Open the game on your new device, tap the avatar at the top-left corner, select Settings, then in the Account section choose the platform where your existing account is linked (e.g. Facebook or Google) and log in. Your account will be transferred to the new device.

title: Forgot password or cannot log in
content:
I forgot my password / can't log in, what should I do?

This depends on which platform your account is linked to. If you forgot your Facebook login password, reset it through Facebook's password recovery process, then log in to the game again with the new password. The same applies for Google or other linked platforms — recover access through that platform first.

title: Playing the same account on multiple devices
content:
Can I play the same account on multiple devices?

Yes, but you can only be logged in on one device at a time. If you log in on a new device, the old device will be kicked off automatically.

title: Purchased a pack but did not receive items
content:
I purchased a pack but didn't receive the items, what should I do?

First, wait a moment as there may be a delay before items arrive. If the items still haven't appeared, go to the in-game customer service and select the 'Purchase not received' category. Submit a screenshot of your payment receipt. You can also email the support team directly. After verification, the support team will re-deliver the items to your account.

title: How to request a refund
content:
How do I request a refund?

Items that have already been consumed in the game are not eligible for refunds. If you have a special refund request, please contact your app store's customer service (Google Play or Apple App Store) to submit a refund application.

title: Game keeps crashing or won't load
content:
The game keeps crashing / won't load, how do I fix it?

First, check your app store to make sure you have the latest version installed. Try reinstalling the game. If the problem persists, please provide your device model, available memory, and if possible, screenshots or a video showing the issue. The technical team will investigate based on the information you provide.

title: Stuck on loading screen after update
content:
I'm stuck on a loading screen after the update, what should I do?

Check your app store to confirm you have the latest version. Try reinstalling the game. If the issue continues, provide your device model, available memory, and any screenshots or video of the problem to customer support. The technical team will review and address the issue.

title: How to block or unblock a player in chat
content:
How do I block or unblock a player in chat?

To block: In the chat interface, tap the player's avatar and select 'Block'.
To unblock: Go to Settings, find the block list, and select the player you want to unblock.

You can also access the block list by clicking the small icon at the top-left corner of the chat window, then scrolling to the Blocked List at the bottom.

title: How to report a player for cheating or harassment
content:
How do I report a player for cheating or harassment?

In the chat interface, tap on the specific chat message and select 'Report'. Provide the reason for your report.
You can also contact in-game moderators, customer service, or the community team with screenshots as evidence to file a report.

title: How Kingdom vs Kingdom (KvK) works
content:
How does Kingdom vs Kingdom (KvK) work? What are the basic rules?

KvK is a cross-server battle feature, also known as 'Crown Invasion'. During this event, several kingdoms are paired together. Players can use the event interface to teleport to other kingdoms, seize their central palace to gain kingdom points, or help defend the palace on their home server.

It's a classic cross-server battle where different alliances in each server have a chance to unite together to fight against other servers. After the event ends, rich rewards are distributed based on each player's contribution.

title: What is the Kill Event and how to prepare
content:
What is the Kill Event and how should I prepare for it?

The Kill Event is a regular event that opens every other week, usually on a specific day. During this event, the goal is to attack other players' castles or resource plots to earn points and claim event rewards. It's a classic PvP gameplay feature.

If you want to attack: Prepare enough resources, troops, and healing speedups for quick recovery. Try to coordinate attacks with your alliance members for better results.

If you prefer to defend: Hide your troops smartly by sending them away to gather, reinforce allies, or join rallies. You can also use a temporary peace shield to protect your castle during the event.

title: Base randomly teleported or moved
content:
I was randomly teleported / my base moved, what happened?

If you were offline for too long, the system may remove your base from the map. When you log back in, it will be assigned to a random location. You can check the kingdom map to find your target location and teleport accordingly. If you have joined an alliance, you can use an Alliance Teleport to fly back to your alliance members.

title: Why food is always at zero
content:
Why do I keep losing food? My food is always at zero.

Troops consume food constantly, and as your army grows the upkeep increases. Here are some tips:

1. Build and upgrade your farms — they constantly produce food.
2. Send troops to gather from resource plots on the kingdom map. Check the troops' load attributes to make sure you gather efficiently.
3. Be careful when gathering — your plots can be attacked by others. If you see someone marching toward your plot, recall your troops immediately.
4. Keep food items in your inventory and only open them when needed — items cannot be looted by enemies.

title: What is a peace shield (bubble) and when to use it
content:
What does a peace shield (bubble) do and when should I use it?

A peace shield is a powerful item that protects your castle from any attack for a set period of time. If you have lots of resources and troops inside your castle and you're anticipating attacks, activating a peace shield will block all incoming attacks.

Keep in mind: while a peace shield is active, you cannot send troops to attack other players either. Use it strategically — for example, when you need to go offline during a Kill Event, or when you're saving up resources for a big upgrade.

title: How Many Shooting Gallery Coins Are Needed
content: How many Shooting Gallery Coins are needed in total? The requirements for the Shooting Gallery fields are 1, 2, 3, 6, 10, 19, 34, 61, and 110 Shooting Gallery Coins. That means you need a total of 246 coins to unlock all fields.

title: Resonance Boost for Lord Equipment
content: How does resonance boost for Lord Equipment work? Resonance has two parts: set resonance and enhancement resonance. Set resonance activates when the required combination of lord equipment pieces is equipped. Enhancement resonance increases when the relevant pieces are enhanced to required levels. If conditions are not met, resonance bonuses do not activate.

title: Resonance for Creation
content: Why is resonance not working for creation equipment? Creation equipment does not use the same resonance rules as standard lord equipment sets. If your setup does not match the resonance requirements shown for that system, no resonance bonus is applied. Verify the exact creation set and enhancement requirements in the equipment interface.

title: Difference between Wounded Conversion and Battlefield Treatment
content: What is the difference between Wounded Conversion and Battlefield Treatment? Battlefield Treatment affects how many of your battle losses are sent to hospitals as wounded. Wounded Conversion is a separate mechanic that converts losses into wounded under specific conditions and limits. They both reduce permanent losses, but they are calculated through different systems and can stack depending on the battle context.
